titleStmt.title: Letter from Patrick F. Quinlan to Fr Provincial Thomas V. Nolan, 4 October 1918
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: P.F. Quinlan

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from Patrick F. Quinlan to Fr. Thomas V Nolan (1867- 1941) regarding a letter received by Quinlan concerning the death of Fr. Fitzgibbon. Fr. Thomas V Nolan became the Provincial of the Irish Province of the Society of Jesus from 1912 to 1922. Besides that, he was a Distribution Committee member responsible for the welfare and distribution of the Belgian refugees who had found refuge in Ireland on account of the World War I.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
68 Middle Abbey Street, Dublin 4th Oct. 1918
re Major the Rev John. F. Fitzgibbon,
S.J. M.L., C.J.
Deceased Dear Fr. Nolan, I have this day receiving a letter from
the war office as follows:- 'Sir
'In reference to your letter of the 23rd
'ultimo, I am directed to transmit to you the accompanying
'Certificate regarding the death of the Reverend
'Fitzgibbon, M,G, temporary Chaplain to the forces,
'3rd Class, and to add that the further subjects mentioned
'in your letter are receiving attention.
'I am, Sir,Your obedient Servant,
(Signed) 'Percy Taylor.'
'for the Secretary' I must now await hearing from them further
as to the arrears of pay to the date of death; the gratuity;
(if any) payable at death, and the kit, I have the papers
ready for swearing but require the information as to
the foregoing before having their service and lodged. Faithfully Yours. P.F. Quinlan A.B. Very Rev. P.V. Nolan, S.J. Provincial, The Presbytery Upper Gardiners St. Dublin
